name: accordion
description: A vertically stacked list of panels driven by server-authoritative open state (WIRE tier). Each panel header is a native <button> inside a heading (role="heading" aria-level), with aria-expanded and aria-controls wired to the panel region. Panel bodies use the HTML `hidden` attribute for collapsed state (removes from the a11y tree + Tab order). Open/close state is a @Wire server fact; the server validates the single/multiple mode invariant and re-renders on every toggle action. Variants: default (bordered card), ghost (dividers only), borderless (flat). No enhancer: keyboard is fully platform-supplied (native button + morph). APG Accordion pattern.
docs: WIRE accordion: open-state is server-authoritative. The AccordionComponent Java class owns the @Wire fields (panels, expandedIds, mode, collapsible) and the toggle() action. The template receives List<Map<String,String>> panels (each with id, title, optional level/disabled), Set<String> expandedIds, mode, collapsible, variant, panelRole. Wire the toggle action via l:click="toggle" on each header button (already in the template). The panel body region for each panel is an empty <div data-slot="accordion-panel"> in the shipped template; the adopter fills it with server-rendered markup in their copy of the template, keyed off the panel id. variant="default" | "ghost" | "borderless". The chevron rotation requires one adopter CSS rule: [data-slot="accordion-header"][data-expanded="true"] .lv-accordion-chevron { transform: rotate(180deg); }. No enhancer: platform native <button> + morph handle all keyboard + focus. APG Accordion pattern, WAI-ARIA compliant.
